preamble: The voiced retroflex fricative [ʐ] (often 'r' in Pinyin) can shift to the lateral [l] in certain Southern Mandarin variants, especially in Taiwan.

examples.note: The 'r' to 'l' shift is a noted feature of Taiwanese accent variants.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
